debts.detail: ancestral reconstruction and Bayesian time-tree workflows have substantive support but still carry incomplete maturity evidence
cross-environment reproducibility is not yet claimed, and external R ecosystem comparisons remain narrower than the broader validation roadmap
evidence: reports/phylogenetic-101/examples/primate-longevity-signal-workflow/accepted-tool-parity demonstrates one checked-in R comparison bundle

debts.detail: publication-grade time-tree conclusions remain unsafe when tip dates, calibrations, or convergence diagnostics fail
evidence: dataset-audit blockers include invalid tip dates and invalid calibrations
bayesian-time-tree is still marked experimental in the limitation registry

debts.detail: This setup block is documented for reproducibility, but it is not an analysis-equivalence target.
This block writes the processed CSV and trimmed tree that the Python evidence pass later consumes.
This is a visual exploration block; the current report does not claim figure-equivalence for base `ape` plots.
These layout variants are tracked as visual surfaces only.
The `phytools::plotTree()` surface is tracked, but no rendered-figure claim is made here.
These `ggtree` examples are tracked as visual surfaces only.
This is a rendered trait-overlay surface and is tracked separately from ordering correctness.
Joined-data `ggtree` figures are tracked here as visual outputs only.
For credibility, the report freezes these R-generated simulation inputs with `set.seed(1)` and reuses the resulting artifacts on both sides.
These are visual teaching plots and are tracked separately from the simulation inputs and signal fits.
The report checks the explicit random-data fit calls and extends the implied checks to the other generated examples.
These are visual inspection surfaces only.
The `bijux-phylogenetics` lambda estimate is within a small numerical tolerance of the R fit.
The side-by-side real-tree versus lambda=0 plots are tracked as visual outputs only.
The covariance surface and lambda-vs-zero test agree within numerical tolerance.
Clade-aligned ancestral point estimates match to floating-point noise.
The Brownian/PIC confidence-interval surface now matches the R reference to floating-point noise.
The ancestral-state branch-color figure is tracked as a visual rendering surface only.
The MRCA clade and the ancestral point estimate match.
The lecture script saves an `.RData` workspace; this report saves explicit machine-readable evidence artifacts instead.

limitations: the report summarizes current checked-in evidence; it does not replace method-specific diagnostics on a new biological dataset
